fields.Text:
Post-op neck pain with Tc-99m MDP uptake greater than Ga-67 uptake. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Degenerative changes}}
fields.Extra: Q1. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: When bone scan shows greater uptake than Gallium-67 in the same region, degenerative disk disease is favored as this denotes prominent osteoblastic activity without significant inflammation. Conversely, when Gallium-67 uptake exceeds or extends beyond bone scan uptake, osteomyelitis due to inflammatory lactoferrin binding at infection sites is favored.

fields.Text:
Scrotal US with enlarged hypervascular epididymis. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Epididymitis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Epididymitis refers to inflammation of the epididymis. Ultrasound features include increased vascularity on color Doppler studies.

fields.Text:
Mid-second-trimester US with membrane-covered midline abdominal wall herniation. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Omphalocele}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q4. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. This herniation is arising from the midline abdominal wall with circumscribed/contained appearance of the herniated structures (red arrows).

fields.Text:
Clear CXR with V/Q scan showing multiple large mismatched defects. PE probability?

{{c1::High probability}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q6. Correct; difficulty: moderate. According to the Modified PIOPED II criteria, high probability of PE is associated with ≥ 2 large mismatched V/Q segmental defects or segmental equivalent defects are present.

fields.Text:
Brain MRI lesion with hemosiderin rim and blooming on GRE/SWI. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Cavernous malformation}}
fields.Extra: Q7. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Small cavernous malformations appear as tiny punctate hypointense foci best seen on gradient echo or susceptibility-weighted imaging, with characteristic "blooming" artifacts from hemosiderin that overestimate actual lesion size.

fields.Text:
Fever and painful swollen eye with postseptal inflammatory change/subperiosteal abscess. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Orbital cellulitis}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q8. Correct; difficulty: not listed. The images show proptosis of the left globe (red arrow) as well as thickening and edema of periorbital soft tissues and a subperiosteal abscess extending posterior to the septum (yellow arrow).

fields.Text:
Gas-filled nuclear medicine device shown. Device?

{{c1::Dose calibrator}}
fields.Extra: Q10. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: A dose calibrator can detect radiation levels over the range of activities that may be encountered clinically, which is usually 10 μCi (0.37 MBq) to 1–2 Ci (37,000–74,000 MBq).

fields.Text:
Cardiac CT with apical thinning and fibrofatty replacement in LAD territory. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Chronic LAD infarction}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q11. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The left coronary artery arises from the left coronary cusp of the aorta and then divides into the left anterior, descending artery and left circumflex artery.

fields.Text:
Stereotactic biopsy diagram with needle above the lesion. Error?

{{c1::Y-axis error}}
fields.Extra: Q12. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: A y-axis/vertical error occurs in stereotactic breast biopsy when the needle is above or below the lesion.

fields.Text:
Coronary CTA with high-grade stenosis in the vessel arising from the right coronary cusp. Vessel?

{{c1::RCA}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q13. Correct; difficulty: moderate. This is a curved multiplanar reformatted coronary CTA.

fields.Text:
CXR with findings classic for sarcoidosis. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Sarcoidosis}}
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: easy. This is a classic image of sarcoidosis.

fields.Text:
Post-ACL repair ovoid anterior intercondylar notch soft tissue. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Arthrofibrosis}}
fields.Extra: Q16. Correct; difficulty: hard. Within the joint space, there is an intermediate signal, ovoid structure at the anterior aspect of the intercondylar notch, most compatible with arthrofibrosis.

fields.Text:
Child with bilateral renal rind-like masses. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Nephroblastomatosis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q17. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Nephroblastomatosis is a form of persistent metanephric blastema in pediatric patients.

fields.Text:
TACE with cystic artery at risk. Prevention of nontarget embolization?

{{c1::Careful distal catheter positioning and slow embolic infusion}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q18. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Nontarget cystic artery embolization is common during TACE since the cystic artery typically arises from the proximal right hepatic artery. Despite frequent occurrence, clinical complications are rare. Most cases are asymptomatic and rarely require cholecystectomy or other intervention.

fields.Text:
Immunocompromised patient with maxillary sinus disease and periantral fat obliteration. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Invasive fungal sinusitis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q19. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The maxillofacial CT shows right maxillary sinus disease with clear obliteration of the anterior periantral fat plane of the right maxillary sinus.

fields.Text:
20-week fetus with isolated choroid plexus cyst. Counseling?

{{c1::Reassurance}}
fields.Extra: Q20. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: A small choroid plexus cyst can be seen in up to 4% of normal pregnancies and typically resolve by 32 weeks, rarely leading adverse outcomes.

fields.Text:
Infant with enlarging head and high-output heart failure from midline vascular malformation. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Vein of Galen malformation}}

1/4
fields.Extra: Q21. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Vein of Galen malformation appears as enlarged median prosencephalic vein dorsal to the tectum. It shows flow voids on MRI and iso/hyperattenuation on CT. MR angiography demonstrates arterial anatomy and fistula site. Associated hydrocephalus and cardiac failure are common in neonates.

fields.Text:
Bacteremia with pulsatile groin mass and vascular outpouching. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Mycotic pseudoaneurysm}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q22. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Mycotic aneurysms are usually a complication of the hematogenous spread of bacterial infection.

fields.Text:
S-sign of Golden from right upper lobe collapse. Cause?

{{c1::Primary bronchogenic carcinoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: easy. The radiograph is an example of the "S-sign of Golden," which is a right upper lobe collapse related to bronchial obstruction from a hilar mass.

fields.Text:
Cervical mass replacing low-signal cervical stroma with restricted diffusion. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Cervical carcinoma}}

1/4
fields.Extra: Q24. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Cervical cancer demonstrates intermediate-to-high T2 signal intensity and restricted diffusion, contrasting with the low-signal fibrous cervical stroma.

fields.Text:
Seizures with Sturge-Weber imaging findings. Expected skin finding?

{{c1::Port-wine stain}}
fields.Extra: Q25. Correct; difficulty: easy. Port wine stain is a facial angiomatous physical exam finding seen in patients with Sturge-Weber syndrome.

fields.Text:
Large expansile intramedullary cord tumor in child/young patient. Diagnosis association?

{{c1::NF1-associated astrocytoma}}

1/6
fields.Extra: Q26. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. The sagittal T1 image shows a large intramedullary lesion expanding the cord (red arrow), which on T2 sequences can be seen as multiple foci of signal abnormality (blue arrows).

fields.Text:
Breast density images. Which density has highest dose for equal thickness?

{{c1::Extremely dense breast tissue}}
fields.Extra: Q28. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Dense breast tissue contains primitive epithelial cells highly susceptible to radiation damage, increases cancer risk, and decreases mammographic sensitivity by masking lesions, requiring consideration of supplemental screening modalities.

fields.Text:
Lower-extremity venous US with prolonged GSV reflux and deep venous reversal. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Deep and superficial venous incompetence}}

1/4
fields.Extra: Q29. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Chronic venous insufficiency is diagnosed by prolonged retrograde flow exceeding 0.5 seconds in superficial veins after augmentation. Respiratory flow reversal in deep veins is highly suspicious for severe deep vein valvular dysfunction.

fields.Text:
Angiography for pancreatitis-related upper GI bleeding. Vessel?

{{c1::Gastroduodenal artery}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q31. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The gastroduodenal artery (GDA - red arrow) is being selectively catheterized here.

fields.Text:
Post-discectomy enhancing epidural tissue at L5/S1. Cause of symptoms?

{{c1::Scar/granulation tissue}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q33. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: The presence of contrast enhancement indicates vascular scar tissue rather than avascular disc material, making this the fundamental imaging principle that allows contrast-enhanced magnetic resonance imaging to reliably distinguish between recurrent herniation and post-surgical fibrosis in failed back surgery syndrome patients.

fields.Text:
Aortic arch variant with left vertebral artery arising directly from arch. Structure?

{{c1::Left vertebral artery}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q34. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Direct origin of the left vertebral artery from the aortic arch is a common asymptomatic variant occurring in up to 8% of the population. Recognition is important for preoperative surgical planning and interventional procedures.

fields.Text:
Pediatric elbow injury with lateral epicondyle avulsion. Mechanism?

{{c1::Varus stress force}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q35. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Although ligamentous injuries in isolation are rare, patients may present with varus or valgus laxity due to overuse or trauma. Tear of the radial collateral ligament (RCL) and associated avulsion fractures are most commonly associated with traumatic varus stress forces. If the traumatic varus stress force is significant enough, additional fracture/dislocation and ligamentous injuries of the elbow can be seen, as in this case.

fields.Text:
Colicky abdominal pain with distended cecum/whirl and paucity of distal gas. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Cecal volvulus}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q36. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The radiographic findings of a gas-filled, distended cecum directed toward the upper abdomen (blue arrows) in a kidney bean or coffee bean shape with whirled mesentery in the right lower quadrant (red arrow), contrast-filled dilated small bowel (yellow arrows), and the relative p

fields.Text:
Fuzzy MDP bone scan after daily uniformity testing. Problem?

{{c1::Incorrect camera energy peak}}
fields.Extra: Q37. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The images show a "fuzzy" appearance to the bones, which is most commonly related to off-peak imaging.

fields.Text:
Compression fracture assessment. Least useful benign-versus-malignant discriminator?

{{c1::Contrast enhancement}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q39. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. The spine is a very common site for metastases and it has been reported that more than 18,000 patients per year are diagnosed with spinal metastatic disease.

fields.Text:
Endometriosis history with extraovarian pelvic mass and enhancing solid component. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Clear cell carcinoma}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q40. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. The right-sided pelvic mass in this patient is separate from the right ovary, but has features of an endometrioma, including a portion of the mass that has high intrinsically high T1 signal on the fat saturated T1-weighted images with associated T2-shading.

fields.Text:
Bronchial/intercostal angiogram with hairpin spinal artery. Structure?

{{c1::Artery of Adamkiewicz}}
fields.Extra: Q41. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Spinal arteries at T4-T5 levels can arise from intercostobronchial trunks and display characteristic hairpin morphology that should be identified on angiography to prevent spinal cord infarction during bronchial artery embolization and other procedures in the vicinity.

fields.Text:
Tiny three-dimensional enhancing breast MRI lesion. BI-RADS term?

{{c1::Enhancing mass}}
fields.Extra: Q42. Correct; difficulty: easy. This refers to a tiny spot of enhancement that can be seen in all three dimensions.

fields.Text:
FDG PET study. Radiotracer decay mode?

{{c1::Positron emission}}
fields.Extra: Q44. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The study shows normal distribution of F-18 fluorodeoxyglucose (FDG), a glucose analog used frequently in oncologic imaging.

fields.Text:
Young woman with pancreatic solid pseudopapillary neoplasm. Possible feature?

{{c1::Peripheral calcification}}
fields.Extra: Q45. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Solid pseudopapillary neoplasm (SPN) is a pancreatic lesion most common in women with low malignant potential. Despite its name, SPN most commonly present as a mixed cystic/solid lesion. Larger SPN (>3 cm) tends to present in the pancreatic body/tail. Peripheral calcifications are most common in lesions >5 cm.

fields.Text:
Splenic trauma with grade III features. AAST grade?

{{c1::Grade III}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q46. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: AAST splenic injury scale is the most widely used splenic trauma grading system on a scale of I to V. One can advance one grade for multiple injuries, each up to grade III. A vascular injury (i.e. pseudoaneurysm or arteriovenous fistula) appears as a focal collection of vascular contrast which decreases in attenuation on delayed images. Active bleeding is defined as a focal or diffuse collection of vascular contrast which increases in size or attenuation on a delayed phase.

fields.Text:
Deceleration trauma with aortic isthmus pseudoaneurysm. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Aortic transection with pseudoaneurysm}}

1/5
fields.Extra: Q48. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Aortic transection with pseudoaneurysm formation typically occurs at the aortic isthmus from deceleration forces. CT angiography shows external aortic contour abnormality requiring emergent intervention when complicated by mediastinal hematoma, hemothorax, or pseudocoarctation.

fields.Text:
Cisternogram with persistent ventricular reflux and delayed convexity flow. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Normal pressure hydrocephalus}}
fields.Extra: Q49. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Normal pressure hydrocephalus shows persistent ventricular reflux at 24 hours without flow over cerebral convexities. Despite historical use for predicting shunt response, cisternography has poor correlation with surgical outcomes and high false-positive rates, limiting its clinical utility for treatment planning.

fields.Text:
Diabetic with chronic flank pain, fevers, enlarged heterogeneous kidney. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Xanthogranulomatous pyelonephritis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q50. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Diffuse renal enlargement in a diabetic with chronic flank pain and low-grade fevers is most suspicious for chronic pyelonephritis or Xanthogranulomatous pyelonephritis of the given options. Contrast CT is generally indicated to evaluate for abscess formation, emphysematous changes, or underlying obstruction that may necessitate drainage or surgical intervention.

fields.Text: On paired Ga-67/Tc-99m MDP imaging, uptake greater on bone scan than gallium favors {{c1::degenerative change}} rather than osteomyelitis.
fields.Extra: Q1. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: When bone scan shows greater uptake than Gallium-67 in the same region, degenerative disk disease is favored as this denotes prominent osteoblastic activity without significant inflammation. Conversely, when Gallium-67 uptake exceeds or extends beyond bone scan uptake, osteomyelitis due to inflammatory lactoferrin binding at infection sites is favored.

fields.Text: Disclosing a financial relationship before discussing a product demonstrates {{c1::management of conflicts of interest}}.
fields.Extra: Q2. Correct; difficulty: easy. The radiologist’s disclosure of their financial affiliation with the company signifies managing conflicts of interest.

fields.Text: Epididymitis on US shows an enlarged epididymis with {{c1::increased color Doppler vascularity}}.
fields.Extra:
Q3. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Epididymitis refers to inflammation of the epididymis. Ultrasound features include increased vascularity on color Doppler studies.

1/2

fields.Text: Omphalocele is a {{c1::midline membrane-covered}} abdominal wall herniation; gastroschisis is usually right-sided and uncovered.
fields.Extra:
Q4. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. This herniation is arising from the midline abdominal wall with circumscribed/contained appearance of the herniated structures (red arrows).

1/3

fields.Text: The most common MRI adverse event reported to the FDA is {{c1::thermal skin burns}}.
fields.Extra: Q5. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Per the FDA, thermal injuries are the most reported MRI adverse event.

fields.Text: Modified PIOPED II high-probability V/Q scan requires {{c1::at least 2 large mismatched segmental defects}} or segmental equivalents.
fields.Extra:
Q6. Correct; difficulty: moderate. According to the Modified PIOPED II criteria, high probability of PE is associated with ≥ 2 large mismatched V/Q segmental defects or segmental equivalent defects are present.

1/3

fields.Text: Cavernous malformations are best seen on GRE/SWI because hemosiderin causes {{c1::blooming susceptibility artifact}}.
fields.Extra: Q7. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Small cavernous malformations appear as tiny punctate hypointense foci best seen on gradient echo or susceptibility-weighted imaging, with characteristic "blooming" artifacts from hemosiderin that overestimate actual lesion size.

fields.Text: Orbital cellulitis extends {{c1::posterior to the orbital septum}}, unlike preseptal cellulitis.
fields.Extra:
Q8. Correct; difficulty: not listed. The images show proptosis of the left globe (red arrow) as well as thickening and edema of periorbital soft tissues and a subperiosteal abscess extending posterior to the septum (yellow arrow).

1/3

fields.Text: Nephrogenic systemic fibrosis primarily involves the {{c1::skin and subcutaneous tissues}}.
fields.Extra: Q9. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Nephrogenic systemic fibrosis most commonly involves the skin, but can also affect internal organs such as skeletal muscle, lungs, and heart

fields.Text: Dose calibrators usually discriminate clinical activities from about {{c1::10 microCi to 1-2 Ci}}.
fields.Extra: Q10. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: A dose calibrator can detect radiation levels over the range of activities that may be encountered clinically, which is usually 10 μCi (0.37 MBq) to 1–2 Ci (37,000–74,000 MBq).

fields.Text: Chronic LAD infarction can show {{c1::apical thinning and fibrofatty replacement}} in the LAD territory.
fields.Extra:
Q11. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The left coronary artery arises from the left coronary cusp of the aorta and then divides into the left anterior, descending artery and left circumflex artery.

1/2

fields.Text: A stereotactic breast biopsy y-axis error means the needle is {{c1::above or below}} the lesion.
fields.Extra: Q12. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: A y-axis/vertical error occurs in stereotactic breast biopsy when the needle is above or below the lesion.

fields.Text: The RCA arises from the {{c1::right coronary cusp}} and travels in the right AV groove.
fields.Extra:
Q13. Correct; difficulty: moderate. This is a curved multiplanar reformatted coronary CTA.

1/2

fields.Text: Bilateral hilar/mediastinal adenopathy in a young Black patient with chronic dyspnea suggests {{c1::sarcoidosis}}.
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: easy. This is a classic image of sarcoidosis.

fields.Text: Round pneumonia in young children is most commonly caused by {{c1::Streptococcus pneumoniae}}.
fields.Extra: Q15. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Round pneumonia typically occurs in young children and usually involves the superior segments of the lower lobes. Air bronchograms may be seen within the opacity. The most common causative organism is Streptococcus pneumoniae.

fields.Text: Arthrofibrosis after ACL repair appears as a low/intermediate signal nodule in the {{c1::anterior intercondylar notch}}.
fields.Extra: Q16. Correct; difficulty: hard. Within the joint space, there is an intermediate signal, ovoid structure at the anterior aspect of the intercondylar notch, most compatible with arthrofibrosis.

fields.Text: Nephroblastomatosis is persistent metanephric blastema and may transform into {{c1::Wilms tumor}}.
fields.Extra:
Q17. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Nephroblastomatosis is a form of persistent metanephric blastema in pediatric patients.

1/2

fields.Text: During right hepatic TACE, nontarget cystic artery embolization is prevented by {{c1::distal catheter positioning and slow embolic infusion}}.
fields.Extra:
Q18. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Nontarget cystic artery embolization is common during TACE since the cystic artery typically arises from the proximal right hepatic artery. Despite frequent occurrence, clinical complications are rare. Most cases are asymptomatic and rarely require cholecystectomy or other intervention.

1/2

fields.Text: Invasive fungal sinusitis is suggested by sinus disease with {{c1::obliteration of periantral fat}} in an immunocompromised patient.
fields.Extra:
Q19. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The maxillofacial CT shows right maxillary sinus disease with clear obliteration of the anterior periantral fat plane of the right maxillary sinus.

1/2

fields.Text: An isolated small choroid plexus cyst at 20 weeks is usually {{c1::incidental and resolves by 32 weeks}}.
fields.Extra: Q20. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: A small choroid plexus cyst can be seen in up to 4% of normal pregnancies and typically resolve by 32 weeks, rarely leading adverse outcomes.

fields.Text: Vein of Galen malformation commonly causes neonatal {{c1::high-output heart failure}} and enlarging head circumference.
fields.Extra:
Q21. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Vein of Galen malformation appears as enlarged median prosencephalic vein dorsal to the tectum. It shows flow voids on MRI and iso/hyperattenuation on CT. MR angiography demonstrates arterial anatomy and fistula site. Associated hydrocephalus and cardiac failure are common in neonates.

1/4

fields.Text: Mycotic aneurysms usually arise from {{c1::hematogenous bacterial infection}} of the arterial wall.
fields.Extra:
Q22. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Mycotic aneurysms are usually a complication of the hematogenous spread of bacterial infection.

1/2

fields.Text: The Golden S sign suggests right upper lobe collapse from central obstruction, classically {{c1::bronchogenic carcinoma}}.
fields.Extra:
Q23. Correct; difficulty: easy. The radiograph is an example of the "S-sign of Golden," which is a right upper lobe collapse related to bronchial obstruction from a hilar mass.

1/2

fields.Text: Cervical cancer appears as intermediate/high T2 signal replacing the low-signal {{c1::fibrous cervical stroma}}.
fields.Extra:
Q24. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Cervical cancer demonstrates intermediate-to-high T2 signal intensity and restricted diffusion, contrasting with the low-signal fibrous cervical stroma.

1/4

fields.Text: Sturge-Weber syndrome is associated with facial {{c1::port-wine stain}}.
fields.Extra: Q25. Correct; difficulty: easy. Port wine stain is a facial angiomatous physical exam finding seen in patients with Sturge-Weber syndrome.

fields.Text: Spinal cord astrocytoma is associated with {{c1::NF1}}.
fields.Extra:
Q26. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. The sagittal T1 image shows a large intramedullary lesion expanding the cord (red arrow), which on T2 sequences can be seen as multiple foci of signal abnormality (blue arrows).

1/6

fields.Text: With fluoroscopic ABC enabled, decreasing electronic magnification leaves image brightness {{c1::unchanged}}.
fields.Extra: Q27. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Decreased magnification with ABC improves SNR - larger field collects more photons while ABC reduces dose rate, distributing noise over greater area.

fields.Text: For equal breast thickness, denser breasts require higher mAs and receive {{c1::higher absorbed dose}}.
fields.Extra: Q28. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Dense breast tissue contains primitive epithelial cells highly susceptible to radiation damage, increases cancer risk, and decreases mammographic sensitivity by masking lesions, requiring consideration of supplemental screening modalities.

fields.Text: Superficial venous incompetence is reflux longer than {{c1::0.5 seconds}} after augmentation.
fields.Extra:
Q29. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Chronic venous insufficiency is diagnosed by prolonged retrograde flow exceeding 0.5 seconds in superficial veins after augmentation. Respiratory flow reversal in deep veins is highly suspicious for severe deep vein valvular dysfunction.

1/4

fields.Text: An isolated adult lesser trochanter avulsion without adequate trauma is suspicious for {{c1::pathologic fracture from malignancy}}.
fields.Extra: Q30. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Isolated lesser trochanter fractures in adults without adequate trauma are highly suspicious for metastatic disease, requiring investigation for occult primary tumors as most cases are associated with underlying malignancy.

fields.Text: The gastroduodenal artery is a common bleeding source in {{c1::duodenal ulcer disease and pancreatitis}}.
fields.Extra:
Q31. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The gastroduodenal artery (GDA - red arrow) is being selectively catheterized here.

1/2

fields.Text: Alteplase has a serum half-life of about {{c1::5 minutes}}; after 5 half-lives, concentration is below 5%.
fields.Extra: Q32. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Alteplase has a serum half-life of about 5 minutes; after 5 half-lives, serum concentration is below 5%.

fields.Text: Postoperative epidural scar enhances because it is {{c1::vascular}}, whereas recurrent disc material is relatively avascular.
fields.Extra:
Q33. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: The presence of contrast enhancement indicates vascular scar tissue rather than avascular disc material, making this the fundamental imaging principle that allows contrast-enhanced magnetic resonance imaging to reliably distinguish between recurrent herniation and post-surgical fibrosis in failed back surgery syndrome patients.

1/2

fields.Text: Direct origin of the left vertebral artery from the aortic arch is a common variant seen in up to {{c1::8%}} of patients.
fields.Extra:
Q34. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Direct origin of the left vertebral artery from the aortic arch is a common asymptomatic variant occurring in up to 8% of the population. Recognition is important for preoperative surgical planning and interventional procedures.

1/2

fields.Text: Radial collateral ligament avulsion/lateral epicondyle injury is most associated with {{c1::varus stress}}.
fields.Extra:
Q35. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Although ligamentous injuries in isolation are rare, patients may present with varus or valgus laxity due to overuse or trauma. Tear of the radial collateral ligament (RCL) and associated avulsion fractures are most commonly associated with traumatic varus stress forces. If the traumatic varus stress force is significant enough, additional fracture/dislocation and ligamentous injuries of the elbow can be seen, as in this case.

1/2

fields.Text: Cecal volvulus often shows a distended cecum with coffee-bean/kidney-bean morphology and {{c1::right lower quadrant whirl}}.
fields.Extra:
Q36. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The radiographic findings of a gas-filled, distended cecum directed toward the upper abdomen (blue arrows) in a kidney bean or coffee bean shape with whirled mesentery in the right lower quadrant (red arrow), contrast-filled dilated small bowel (yellow arrows), and the relative p

1/2

fields.Text: Off-peak gamma camera imaging produces fuzzy bone scan images when the energy peak is not set to {{c1::Tc-99m's 140-keV photopeak}}.
fields.Extra: Q37. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The images show a "fuzzy" appearance to the bones, which is most commonly related to off-peak imaging.

fields.Text: ACR Appropriateness Criteria are developed and maintained by the {{c1::American College of Radiology}}.
fields.Extra: Q38. Correct; difficulty: easy. The American College of Radiology (ACR) has developed a group of documents known as the ACR Appropriateness Criteria.

fields.Text: Contrast enhancement is least useful for benign versus malignant compression fracture because {{c1::both acute/subacute fractures can enhance}}.
fields.Extra:
Q39. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. The spine is a very common site for metastases and it has been reported that more than 18,000 patients per year are diagnosed with spinal metastatic disease.

1/2

fields.Text: Endometriosis increases risk for {{c1::clear cell and endometrioid}} epithelial ovarian cancers.
fields.Extra:
Q40. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. The right-sided pelvic mass in this patient is separate from the right ovary, but has features of an endometrioma, including a portion of the mass that has high intrinsically high T1 signal on the fat saturated T1-weighted images with associated T2-shading.

1/3

fields.Text: The artery of Adamkiewicz has a characteristic {{c1::hairpin turn}} and supplies the anterior spinal cord.
fields.Extra: Q41. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Spinal arteries at T4-T5 levels can arise from intercostobronchial trunks and display characteristic hairpin morphology that should be identified on angiography to prevent spinal cord infarction during bronchial artery embolization and other procedures in the vicinity.

fields.Text: In BI-RADS MRI version 6, a tiny enhancing space-occupying lesion visible in 3 dimensions is an {{c1::enhancing mass}}.
fields.Extra: Q42. Correct; difficulty: easy. This refers to a tiny spot of enhancement that can be seen in all three dimensions.

fields.Text: Routine spatial resolution from lowest to highest is {{c1::MRI, CT, radiography}}.
fields.Extra: Q43. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Digital radiographs generally have a limiting resolution of three-line pairs per millimeter.

fields.Text: F-18 FDG decays by {{c1::positron emission}}.
fields.Extra: Q44. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The study shows normal distribution of F-18 fluorodeoxyglucose (FDG), a glucose analog used frequently in oncologic imaging.

fields.Text: Solid pseudopapillary neoplasm of the pancreas can show {{c1::peripheral calcification}}, especially when larger than 5 cm.
fields.Extra: Q45. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Solid pseudopapillary neoplasm (SPN) is a pancreatic lesion most common in women with low malignant potential. Despite its name, SPN most commonly present as a mixed cystic/solid lesion. Larger SPN (>3 cm) tends to present in the pancreatic body/tail. Peripheral calcifications are most common in lesions >5 cm.

fields.Text: AAST grade III splenic injury includes laceration greater than {{c1::3 cm}} depth or subcapsular hematoma greater than 50% surface area.
fields.Extra:
Q46. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: AAST splenic injury scale is the most widely used splenic trauma grading system on a scale of I to V. One can advance one grade for multiple injuries, each up to grade III. A vascular injury (i.e. pseudoaneurysm or arteriovenous fistula) appears as a focal collection of vascular contrast which decreases in attenuation on delayed images. Active bleeding is defined as a focal or diffuse collection of vascular contrast which increases in size or attenuation on a delayed phase.

1/2

fields.Text: Increasing MRI matrix size from 256 to 512 at the same FOV {{c1::improves spatial resolution}}.
fields.Extra: Q47. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Increasing matrix size (with other parameters held equal) generally results in improved spatial resolution.

fields.Text: Traumatic aortic transection with pseudoaneurysm most often occurs at the {{c1::aortic isthmus}}.
fields.Extra:
Q48. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Aortic transection with pseudoaneurysm formation typically occurs at the aortic isthmus from deceleration forces. CT angiography shows external aortic contour abnormality requiring emergent intervention when complicated by mediastinal hematoma, hemothorax, or pseudocoarctation.

1/5

fields.Text: Normal pressure hydrocephalus cisternography shows persistent ventricular reflux at {{c1::24 hours}} with poor convexity flow.
fields.Extra: Q49. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Normal pressure hydrocephalus shows persistent ventricular reflux at 24 hours without flow over cerebral convexities. Despite historical use for predicting shunt response, cisternography has poor correlation with surgical outcomes and high false-positive rates, limiting its clinical utility for treatment planning.

fields.Text: Xanthogranulomatous pyelonephritis is chronic renal infection, often in diabetics, associated with {{c1::stones/obstruction}}.
fields.Extra:
Q50. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Diffuse renal enlargement in a diabetic with chronic flank pain and low-grade fevers is most suspicious for chronic pyelonephritis or Xanthogranulomatous pyelonephritis of the given options. Contrast CT is generally indicated to evaluate for abscess formation, emphysematous changes, or underlying obstruction that may necessitate drainage or surgical intervention.

1/2

fields.Text: Ga-67 uptake greater than or extending beyond bone scan uptake favors {{c1::osteomyelitis}}, while bone uptake greater than gallium favors degeneration.
fields.Extra: Q1. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: When bone scan shows greater uptake than Gallium-67 in the same region, degenerative disk disease is favored as this denotes prominent osteoblastic activity without significant inflammation. Conversely, when Gallium-67 uptake exceeds or extends beyond bone scan uptake, osteomyelitis due to inflammatory lactoferrin binding at infection sites is favored.

fields.Text: Omphalocele is membrane-covered and midline; {{c1::gastroschisis}} is uncovered and usually right-sided.
fields.Extra:
Q4. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. This herniation is arising from the midline abdominal wall with circumscribed/contained appearance of the herniated structures (red arrows).

1/3

fields.Text: MRI projectile injuries are dramatic but uncommon; the most reported MRI adverse events are {{c1::thermal skin burns}}.
fields.Extra: Q5. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Per the FDA, thermal injuries are the most reported MRI adverse event.

fields.Text: In an immunocompromised patient, periantral fat obliteration with sinus disease suggests {{c1::invasive fungal sinusitis}} rather than allergic fungal sinusitis.
fields.Extra:
Q19. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The maxillofacial CT shows right maxillary sinus disease with clear obliteration of the anterior periantral fat plane of the right maxillary sinus.

1/2

fields.Text: Spinal cord astrocytoma is associated with {{c1::NF1}}, while ependymoma is more central and may have a cap sign/syrinx.
fields.Extra:
Q26. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. The sagittal T1 image shows a large intramedullary lesion expanding the cord (red arrow), which on T2 sequences can be seen as multiple foci of signal abnormality (blue arrows).

1/6

fields.Text: Automatic brightness control keeps fluoroscopic image brightness {{c1::unchanged}} when electronic magnification is decreased.
fields.Extra: Q27. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Decreased magnification with ABC improves SNR - larger field collects more photons while ABC reduces dose rate, distributing noise over greater area.

fields.Text: Deep venous respiratory flow reversal plus prolonged GSV reflux means {{c1::both deep and superficial venous incompetence}}.
fields.Extra:
Q29. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Chronic venous insufficiency is diagnosed by prolonged retrograde flow exceeding 0.5 seconds in superficial veins after augmentation. Respiratory flow reversal in deep veins is highly suspicious for severe deep vein valvular dysfunction.

1/4

fields.Text: After 5 half-lives, drug concentration is less than 5%; for alteplase this is about {{c1::25 minutes}}.
fields.Extra: Q32. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Alteplase has a serum half-life of about 5 minutes; after 5 half-lives, serum concentration is below 5%.

fields.Text: A distended cecum with RLQ whirl and paucity of distal gas favors {{c1::cecal volvulus}}.
fields.Extra:
Q36. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The radiographic findings of a gas-filled, distended cecum directed toward the upper abdomen (blue arrows) in a kidney bean or coffee bean shape with whirled mesentery in the right lower quadrant (red arrow), contrast-filled dilated small bowel (yellow arrows), and the relative p

1/2

fields.Text: Contrast enhancement is not a strong discriminator for compression fracture etiology because {{c1::benign and malignant acute/subacute fractures may enhance}}.
fields.Extra:
Q39. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. The spine is a very common site for metastases and it has been reported that more than 18,000 patients per year are diagnosed with spinal metastatic disease.

1/2

fields.Text: An enhancing solid component in an endometrioma raises concern for malignant transformation, especially {{c1::clear cell carcinoma}}.
fields.Extra:
Q40. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. The right-sided pelvic mass in this patient is separate from the right ovary, but has features of an endometrioma, including a portion of the mass that has high intrinsically high T1 signal on the fat saturated T1-weighted images with associated T2-shading.

1/3

fields.Text: Solid pseudopapillary neoplasm is most common in young women and may show {{c1::peripheral calcification}}.
fields.Extra: Q45. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Solid pseudopapillary neoplasm (SPN) is a pancreatic lesion most common in women with low malignant potential. Despite its name, SPN most commonly present as a mixed cystic/solid lesion. Larger SPN (>3 cm) tends to present in the pancreatic body/tail. Peripheral calcifications are most common in lesions >5 cm.
